title: Command-line input validation: reject unused args
body:
Several podman commands accept no subcommands. Some
of those were not actually checking, though, which
could lead to user confusion. Added validation where
missing; and, refactored to minimize duplication.

(Side note: I decided against using cobra.NoArgs
because its error message, "unknown command",
misleadingly implies that there are known ones).

Also added validation to varlink

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Hi @edsantiago. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
LGTM, @mheon should we merge or wait after the release?

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)

At this point I'd prefer to wait... Though I don't know what the status on the buildah 1.7.1 vendor is, so we might have time
New force-push: added `pod create` and `refresh`. I hope that's all of them.
Argh. @mheon should `renumber` include this validation? (Found on my fourth pass, because `renumber` isn't documented under --help)
Wait, renumber's not in --help? That sounds like a bug
And yes, this is a good thing for renumber
It's in `system --help`, which I hadn't looked at. K, thanks.
i would like a chance to review this before someone merges it ... give me until tomorrow and I will merge it once the release and etc is done.
force-push: rebased on master; added container-prune and image-prune
